phonetic: k蓴藧藞te蕭; k蓴藧藞te瑟蕭
examples: He ordered the cortege to stop.
It begins with what I always hear as a kind of funeral cortege idea.
The procession passes serenely, like a cortege entering the cemetery gates.
Forty-five minutes later, four black horses with black plumes lead the McLaren cortege along the street.
The cortege transporting the 22-year-old's body passed through Wootton Bassett in Wiltshire this afternoon.
